There is no hold on the jerk this week. That means we want to apply what we have worked on for the last three weeks. Do not get lazy with the jerk

title: Skill
description: A. Combo progression strict pull up + bar muscle ups.  Week 3 of 3Perform a number of strict pull ups of your choice but save enough energy to allow you to do 5-10 BMU.Rest as needed x 3 sets. Try to build off of last week with either the strict pull ups or bmu.option 2 : BMU practice 3x max setB. 10 minutes of Handstand ramp practice with P-Bars

title: Power Clean and Jerks
description: Every 2:00 x 5 Sets 3 Power Cleans + 1 Jerks (75+%)*begin at 75% of weaker lift and build to a heavy complex for the day (without movement quality suffering). You may go directly from power clean into the jerk today if able.

title: HSPU Work
description: 10-8-6 (Deficit) Strict HSPU3 x 10 (Deficit) Kipping HSPU*I would like you to attempt to hit your 10 SHSPU at your ending deficit from Jan 13 (set of 8) and build deficit as able to from there *you may scale as low as 6-5-4 reps today for regular SHSPU. If you need to go less than that than I would prefer we go to box SHSPU to build strength and capacity in that manner
notes: No deficit SHSPU unbrokenSuper happy how these went3x10 4”

title: WB/TTB/BBJ
description: 20:00 AMRAP21 Wall Balls (20/14)15 TTB9 Burpee Box Jumps (24/20")Very Open style workout! Start out smooth on this. The sticking point is going to be your abs and shoulders on the TTB and Burpee Box Jumps. Stay consistent on the BBJ so that you can transition fairly quickly back to the Wall Balls. NOTE: It is burpee box jumps not box jump overs so make sure you stand all the way up on the box.

title: Optional Extra
description: 500 Double Unders for time[8:00 Cap]

title: Bike/HPC/Bike
description: 5 Sets for total time:20/15 Cal Fan Bike10 Hang Power Cleans (185/125)20/15 Cal Fan Bike5-4-3-2-1 Hang Power Cleans**1:00 Rest between sets. **HPC weights:5: 70% 4: 75%3: 80%2: 85%1: 90%*So for set 1, you do Bike, 10 HPC, Bike, 5 HPC. Set 2 you will end with 4 HPC. (you do NOT do 5-4-3-2-1 every single time). The rest period will basically be enough to allow you to get your barbell weight back to the set of 10 weight. Time stops when you finish your 1 Rep at 90%Scaling: The set of 10 HPC should be around 60-65% of your 1RM HPC.

title: Skill EMOM
description: 15:00 EMOMMin 1) 2-7 RMUMin 2) 3-8 Deficit Kipping HSPUMin 3) 15/10 Cal Ski*pick a difficult rep scheme (and depth for HSPU), if necessary you are allowed to reduce reps if you get too aggressive, but I want you to test how well you know your body and how close to your potential you can push yourself without me telling you the exact reps to do

title: Bench Clusters (Wk 6)
description: 4 Sets:2 Reps15sec rest2 Reps15sec rest2 reps2-3min rest(80-85%)

title: Crossfit.Com
description: 12:00 AMRAP24 DB Power Snatches (50/35)6 Burpee Pullups*pick a pullup bar that is 12 inches above your reach.*I ALMOST scaled this workout up to Burpee BMU, but then I decided that that was being too nice to you guys because that would allow you to go slower. Lets see who can push it today.

title: Row/Thruster/CTB
description: 14:00 AMRAP30/24 Cal Row30 Thrusters (75/55)30 Chest to Bar Pullups*Row at a strong pace, but one that is going to allow you to get off the rower and start up on the thrusters within 15 seconds. Yes, the thruster is light. It is meant to make you do more reps than you want to.Scaling:If the CTB is going to hold you back a lot, I want you to pick between:a. 30/24 Cal Row-25 Thrusters-20 CTBb. 30/24 Cal Row-20 Thrusters-10 CTB*pick something that is going to allow you to move at a good clip for all 14 minutes

title: PC+J
description: Every 1:30 x 3 Sets:3 Power Cleans + 1 Push Jerk(70-72.5-75%)immediately intoEvery 1:30 x 3 Sets:2 Power Cleans + 1 Push Jerk(77.5-80-82.5%)immediately intoEvery 1:30 x 3 Sets:1 Power Clean + 1 Push Jerk (85-87.5-90%)*percent based off PC*You must drop and reset power cleans, however I want your hands back on the bar as quick as possible*You may drive right into the jerk like last week or reset if needed

title: Run/DL/WW/Run
description: For Time:800m Runthen,5-5-5-5-5 Deadlift (365/255)5-4-3-2-1 Wall Walksthen,800m Run*Deadlift: Scale to 75% of 1RM if you dont think you can do 5s unbroken

title: Run and Pull
description: 20:00 AMRAP400m Run15 Chest to Bar Pullups*you should be running a pace that allows you to get back into the gym and jump on that bar within 10 seconds tops. I want consistent rounds throughout that allows you to finish hard on the last round.*Scale:Scale reps to something you can go unbroken or 2 sets for most rounds (scale to regular pullups if you need to scale to less than 8)

title: Snatch Work
description: 3:00 EMOM2 Hang Power Snatches (75% of 1RM HPS)immediately into3:00 EMOMPower Snatch + Hang Power Snatch (80% of 1RM PS)immediately into5:00 EMOM1 Power Snatch + 1 Snatch *build off of 80% to a heavy set for the dayimmediately into5:00 EMOM1-2 Snatches at 100% of your heavy PS+S(drop and reset if you are able to do 2 reps)*The 1-2 rep option for snatches will be based on your power v. full. If your power is very close to your full snatch, you will likely need to do 1 rep each minute, but if your power snatch is 80-90% of your 1RM snatch, then do 2 reps per minute.

You get 4:00 to complete each of these *If no RMU, do strict dips today to work pressing strength. If no BMU, you can choose between strict pullups or jumping BMU
notes: Scaled 20 step back, back rack lunges 135

title: Clean Work
description: 3:00 EMOM2 Power Cleans (75% of 1RM PC-drop and reset)immediately into3:00 EMOM1 Power Clean + 1 Hang Power Clean (80%)immediately into5:00 EMOM1 Power Clean + Clean*build off the 80% to a heavy set for the day (drop and reset between the PC and Clean)immediately into5:00 EMOM1-2 Cleans (100% of your heavy set from the previous EMOM)*just like Monday, the 1-2 reps should be decided based on how you are feeling by the end of this along with how your power clean compares to your full clean
notes: Dropped in weight form was bad

title: Clean Work
description: Every 1:30 x 4 Sets:1 Push Press + 1 Split Jerk (90% of your heaviest Push Press + Split Jerk set from last week)Rest an extra minute after set 4 then,Every 2:00 x 4 Sets:1 Pause Jerk Dip + 1 Jerk Drive (100-110% of 1RM Jerk)***Rest an extra minute after set 4 then,Every 2:00 x 4 Sets:1-2 Jerks (90% of heaviest set from last weeks split jerk EMOM)*pick 1 or 2 reps based on how you are feeling today ***Pause jerk dip-pause bottom of dip for 1 full secondJerk Drive-dip (no pause) and drive the bar up like you are going to do a jerk to about chin or eye height Goal is to have your pause dip and the dip of your jerk drive look the exact same as far as positioning goes (and then we apply that same look to our split jerks)

description: 21-15-9Dual KB Hang Power Snatches (55s/35s)BMU[12:00 Cap]Scaling:KB HPS: Option 1: scale weight to something you can do for 15+ reps when fresh. Option 2: if you are someone that really struggles with overhead mobility, then doing single arm KB HPS is a great option to make sure you are hitting the desired OH position rather than forcing two KBs overhead and having them out in front of you every rep. BMU: Rx is 45 reps. When looking at this workouts cap and considering how to scale reps, we should be looking at about 3-5 minutes of KB work, leaving 7-9 minutes for BMU. How many reps could you do in 7:00 when fresh? For example, if you think its around 20, then I would want you to do 8-6-4 or 9-7-5 today.

title: Team Saturday!
description: Team of 2: 12:00 AMRAP of alternating rounds 30 Double Unders10 Power Snatch (75/55)Goal is to move fast knowing you have the rest while your partner goes. You are allowed to muscle snatch!Scaling: Should be a very manageable, repeatable rep count of DU. If you are newer to DU, you can choose to do 30 total jumps (so if you did single, single, double, single that would be 4 reps). For the Snatch, you should think you could do at least 5 rounds unbroken before needing to break a set of 10. You are allowed to scale reps down to 8 or 6 before lowering the weight.

title: Machine Intervals

title: Tempo Snatch Pulls

title: High Hang Power Snatches
description: Every 2:00 x 4 Sets:2 High Hang Power Snatch + 2 Hang Power Snatches (65%)Again, you should continue to focus on keeping the bar in your hip crease during the dip on the high hang and then snapping under the bar as fast as you can. Then we will increase the ROM with the hang power snatches. You should be trying to end up in the same high hang position so that you apply your first 2 reps to your second 2 reps!

title: Overhead Complex
description: Every 2:00 x 4 Sets:1 Pause Jerk Dip + 1 Push Press + 1 Jerk Dip + 1 Split Jerk (70% of 1RM Split Jerk) *Pause for 1 full second in first dip. You will reset standing tall between each rep. Your goal is to have the bottom position of your dip look the exact same for all 4 reps. The push press is going to be a little heavy as we are going off our split jerk, but then you should be getting under that bar quick on the split jerks.

title: 2 Parter!
description: EMOM to Failure (10:00 Cap)25 Drag Rope Double Unders1-3 Wall Walks*in same minute -2:00 Rest after you fail or complete 10:00 then-Rope Climbs For Time: (3 options)A. 10 Legless + 10 Regular Rope Climbs B. 10-20 Regular Rope Climbs for timeC. 10 Rope Climbs not for time, focusing on big, efficient pulls and descents*pick a rep scheme for part 2 that you are completing in under 10 minutes*for part 1, you can sub in 25-50 regular DU or 50 single unders (you should be picking a challenging rep scheme that allows you to get through at least 5 minutes)

title: Speed Hang Power Cleans

DL + 3 HS High Pull + 3 Hang Muscle Snatch + 3 Snatch Push Press + 3 Snatch Grip Push Jerks 5 Box Jumps

title: Snatch Complex

You should be picking a weight that is difficult but unbroken for at least 3 rounds (and then 2 sets at most if you do end up breaking it up)

You HAVE to avoid going to failure! Pick a sled weight that is a slow trudge (tough unbroken on round one and then 1-2 quick stops on round 2-4)Part 2: Pure grit. Can you jump with those legs feeling like concrete??Scaling:SHSPU: should be something you can complete in 1-3 sets every single round. You must not be going to failure on this or its going to be a lot of standing around. Start by decreasing depth. If 9 with no deficit cant be done in 3 sets, then you need to scale to 3-6 reps.Sled: If no sled, you will do a 50yd sandbag bear hug carry!

title: Rowing Challenge
description: 22:00 AMRAP250/200m Row*10 Burpees for each second above your given pace.*You must row 5 seconds faster than your 5k pace. Ie if you rowed a 2:00 pace for 5k you must row 1:55.0-1:55.9 (this is going to force you to finish your sets....if you coast and your time is 1:56.0+ you are penalized). You must do your burpees before you can do another row *Set your monitor to 250/200m intervals with unlimited rest. Rest as needed between sets. Your goal is to get as many sets of 250/200m as you can without getting any penalties. Score is the number of sets you get in.

title: WORLD DIABETES DAY
description: "Kaceys Metabolic Mayhem"Buy in: 2002m bike/row5.7 rounds (155/103)5 rounds3 power snatch21 bar facing burpees3 squat cleans".7" round 2 power snatch15 bar facing burpees2 squat cleanCash out: 2002m row/bikeExplanation:5.7(for the rounds) is the range for your a1c before you enter pre-diabetic. Those with diabetes are constantly trying to get their A1c down to that level.The switch between heavy weights and high volume aerobic motions is meant to simulate what it feels like during a blood sugar high and/or low.Kacey was diagnosed 2/15/2002, so 2002m for the year and 2/15/2 for the ".7" round.
